name: New Mexico (United States)

colors.symbolism: the gold of the Spanish conquistadors and the colors of old Spain
the blood of the Spanish conquistadors and the colors of old Spain

description: A solid yellow field charged in the center with a red Zia sun symbol — a circle from which radiate four groups of four straight rays in the cardinal directions.
history: Adopted on 15 March 1925 from a design by Dr. Harry Mera, a Santa Fe physician and archaeologist whose wife Reba sewed the prototype. Mera based the central emblem on a sun design found on a late-19th-century water jar from Zia Pueblo. The colors recall the banner of Castile carried by Spanish conquistadors. The flag has consistently been ranked one of the best-designed U.S. state flags by the North American Vexillological Association.

features.symbolism: the red and yellow of old Spain, recalling New Mexico's heritage as part of the Spanish Empire and later Mexico

an ancient sun sign of the Zia Pueblo: four groups of four rays in the cardinal directions, representing the four directions, the four seasons, the four times of day, and the four stages of life — bound together by the sacred number four and the circle of life
